title: Shinjis love of astronomy sign of an asshole
display_title: Shinjis love of astronomy sign of an asshole
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's love of astronomy: sign of an asshole?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 06, 2013 5:26 pm [Split from after this post in "3.33 Things you noticed". - Monk] How liking to see the stars is supposed to makes Shinji look like an asshole?

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Jul 06, 2013 8:54 pm Thanks for the link Kendrix! But like ElMariachi I don´t understand that theory at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jul 07, 2013 10:28 am kuribo-04 wrote: Thanks for the link Kendrix! But like ElMariachi I don´t understand that theory at all.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 07, 2013 2:06 pm Kendrix wrote: It's not like I ever thought it made sense, but there were ppl who spun Kaworu's ensuing "void" comment to imply "The rotten bastard totally enjoyed ruining the planet so it will be empty and unchanging like space!" Because everybody who things outer space is awesome wants to live there, you see... /sarcarm. This is actually a deep observation on that faraway fascination with nothingness/death/destruction that many humans have - including the audience, obviously, or why else in fucks name would you be watching one of these apocalyptic sci-fi movies?

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Jul 07, 2013 2:47 pm Sometimes I think that Kaworu just wants to help Shinji, sometimes I think he is just crazy (maybe evil?). Did you know that he is (apparently) based on the creator of the Sailor Moon anime (Ikuhara I think)? That would make 3.0, just like ep. 24 a story of interaction between two real people (Ikuhara-Anno). Pretty awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sun Jul 07, 2013 2:48 pm I don't think that's it. That's just Anno's love for overinterpretation of simplest things.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Jul 07, 2013 3:01 pm ^ True! He always does that, even in interviews. I wanted to add something about the "void world commentary". I myself fear change pretty much, too much maybe. Maybe Anno does too, that would explain the desire to make a film in which his self-insert awakes after having "slept" 14 years. And we see that this makes Shinji nervous (maybe he should have suffered more, I know I would go crazy, but that moment was left for the second part of the film). And since Anno has this strange habit of criticizing everything that he does or thinks, I kind of see that scene as Kaworu (his subconscious/better self) telling him "Oh, you fear change. That makes you a piece of shit, you prefer a void world, death, etc." Anno is like that.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 07, 2013 6:04 pm thegoodson wrote: Shinji just keeps on smiling while Kaworu says that shit too! He's not even like "Jesus dude, I like stars, fuck off." But I think it shows that Shinji's kind of oblivious to the bigger picture of not only how the world works, but why he does things. He's never fully aware of where his motivations are coming from.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jul 08, 2013 8:42 am Stillborn wrote: I don't think that's it. That's just Anno's love for overinterpretation of simplest things.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 08, 2013 8:53 am My reading of that scene was less that "liking astronomy makes you an asshole" and more "Kaworu is linking what he already knows about Shinji to another facet of him". After all, Kaworu has known about Shinji since long before they met, including his personality ("You never change"), and said he's "always thinking about" him. The entire explanation has more of an air of Kaworu explaining to Shinji why he likes a certain thing, based on other knowledge and observations, than of Kaworu drawing a conclusion from just that one piece of evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jul 08, 2013 10:20 am In other words, "understanding" taking place. Judging by what he says next, he must've been thinking something like, "Wow, the third child is even more interesting in person."

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Jul 08, 2013 12:25 pm Kendrix wrote: In other words, "understanding" taking place. Judging by what he says next, he must've been thinking something like, "Wow, the third child is even more interesting in person."

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jul 08, 2013 12:51 pm Imagine he have little altar dedicated to Shinji, with hundreds of pictures, including those from bathroom, like some kind of a stalker XD

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Mon Jul 08, 2013 12:57 pm @Stillborn Then he's shouting every night: "khyaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!! Shin-chan ur so kawaii, uguu!!" Wait, what was this thread about? Oh yeah, no, i don't think it's sign of an asshole if you love astronomy. X)

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jul 08, 2013 1:02 pm Actually no. Astronomy is normal hobby. One may just like to look at pretty shiny things in the sky. Often it's that simple. But this is Eva and this Shinji. Everything HE does must point that HE is in some way faulted and selfish or a brat.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jul 08, 2013 2:11 pm on the subject of Anno's hobbies and how they transfer to the characters: Remember that same documentary? Kid: "Do you like the anime you make?" Anno: "There's parts I like, and parts I don't like" Kid: "Which are the ones you don't like?" Anno: "The one's I'm in." ...Shinji and the Cello, anyone? --- This already shows up in episode 11, where we see Shinji marvelling at the sky. Asuka's "...but the city looks so lifeless without the artificial lights" could be seen as a similar point of sorts. Excpecially considering that city DOES blow up there, too. Ah, and look, Shinji and Rei having a philosophic conversation ~<3

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 09, 2013 7:30 pm After a closer inspection, I think that Kaworu's line about Shinji's preference for a "void and merciless abyss of a world" didn't came from the fact that Shinji like to see the stars, but by his explanation og why he likes to see them : 00:46:36 {Shinji} Whenever I think about how vast the universe is, 00:46:39 {Shinji} it's always put my mind completely at ease, ever since I was little. 00:46:44 {Shinji} I guess I'm happy that it hasn't changed at all these past fourteen years. 00:46:49 {Shinji} It makes me feel like I can stop worrying about myself and really calm down. 00:46:55 {Shinji} Uh, it's hard to put into words.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Jul 09, 2013 7:49 pm ElMariachi wrote: After a closer inspection, I think that Kaworu's line about Shinji's preference for a "void and merciless abyss of a world" didn't came from the fact that Shinji like to see the stars, but by his explanation og why he likes to see them : Basically his love for stargazing are one of the hints of his distaste of change, and Kaworu, perfectly knowing Shinji, noted it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jul 10, 2013 3:01 am Oh I think that he already know Shinji's distaste for change before that, like he said he's always thinking about him, but he probably chose the moment they were stargazing to notice to Shinji that quirk he have because their friendship was already settled : telling him that he prefers a "void and merciless abyss of a world" when he proposed to play a duet with him, which was the fist time they met, would have been really rude and gained him Shinji's antipathy, he's not EMK!

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 10, 2013 3:09 am Try as I may, I can't connect a degree of caution about change (which is all it amounts too - life imposes change continuously, after all) with the remark about liking a "void and merciless abyss of a world", which is surely about seeing the immensity of space and our utter insignificance alongside it.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Jul 06, 2013 8:54 pm Thanks for the link Kendrix! But like ElMariachi I
